creative_brief: Hero image: Rachel on her Kempsey farm at golden hour, looking directly into camera with quiet conviction — not smiling for the lens, but present. Below the fold, intercut close-up product shots (Bundarra salami, Paris Creek dairy, Collins seafood on ice) with candid farmer portraits. Earthy palette, no gloss. Typography in sentence case, generous whitespace. Feels like a letter, not a billboard.
headline: I stopped pretending supermarkets were feeding my family well
subhead: I'm Rachel. I left acting to raise cattle on regenerative pastures in Kempsey, NSW. Then I saw what happens to honest food once it enters the supply chain — and I couldn't unsee it. So I helped build a grocery store with zero middlemen between the farm and your kitchen table.
hero_copy:
You know that quiet guilt at the checkout? Where you read the label, see "product of Australia," and still have no idea who grew it, how they raised it, or how long it's been sitting in a warehouse?

I felt it too. Except I was also the farmer watching my neighbours sell beautiful produce at a loss — while families paid premium prices for food that had been cold-stored for weeks.

FarmThru exists because both sides of that equation are broken. And fixing it turned out to be simpler than anyone expected.

sections.heading: The problem isn't your choices — it's what's missing from the shelf
sections.body:
When I started farming in Kempsey, I assumed the hard part was the land. Regenerative pastures, rotational grazing, doing it right. It wasn't. The hard part was watching good food disappear into a supply chain that strips value from every person it touches.

Farmers get squeezed. Wholesalers take their margin. Distributors take theirs. By the time it reaches you, the pork belly from Bundarra Farm or the wild-caught salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast would have passed through hands that added cost but nothing else.

FarmThru removes every one of those hands. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen. You order online. Food comes direct from farms like Paris Creek in South Australia — carbon-neutral, bio-dynamic dairy — or eggs from Farmer Brown in NSW. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday, on your schedule.

This isn't a subscription box. It's a real grocery store built on a supply chain short enough that we can show you exactly where your food comes from.

sections.heading: Why I need you to know this isn't charity
sections.body:
People hear "regenerative" and think expensive. They hear "farm-direct" and think farmers' market prices on a Saturday morning.

Here's what actually happens when you cut every middleman: farmers earn more, and you pay fairly. Not more. Fairly. Because the margin that used to feed a supply chain now feeds the people who grew your food — and keeps your price honest.

Little Yarran Farm in NSW doesn't need a marketing department. Bundarra doesn't need a distributor for their pork and fennel salami. They need a direct line to families who care. That's what FarmThru is.

No commitment. No lock-in. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. Try it once. If the freshness doesn't speak for itself, you've lost nothing.

sections.heading: We're building this together — and you can own a piece of it
sections.body:
FarmThru is opening an equity crowdfunding campaign. That means you don't just shop with us — you can be a partner in a food system that works differently.

Join the waitlist with a fully refundable $5 deposit and you'll be first to know when the campaign goes live. No obligation. No pressure. Just a seat at the table before it fills up.

This is early. The risks are real — like any growing business, outcomes aren't guaranteed and you could lose what you invest. But if you've ever stood in a supermarket aisle and thought "there has to be something better" — this is your chance to help build it.

Be part of it. Not because we need your money. Because this kind of grocery store shouldn't belong to a corporation. It should belong to the people who eat the food.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. This page is not a financial product offer. Any investment in FarmThru will carry risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. The $5 waitlist deposit is fully refundable at any time. You should consider whether this type of investment is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent financial advice if needed.
